_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to deliver structured spoken texts for particular purposes, demonstrating different levels of formality and using features of voice, visual elements and content selection effectively.

_source.w: We are learning to deliver structured spoken texts for particular purposes, demonstrating different levels of formality and using features of voice, visual elements and content selection effectively.
y: Commanding a range of formality registers in spoken presentations demonstrates communicative versatility.
Effective content selection and delivery skills are essential for academic, professional and civic life.
These skills help us advocate for ourselves and others in any speaking situation.
eg: We deliver a formal spoken text — like a policy proposal or research presentation — with appropriate register.
We demonstrate the ability to shift formality levels within a presentation when appropriate.
We select content strategically, including only what serves our purpose and audience.
We use vocal features and visual elements that enhance rather than distract from our message.
We adapt our presentation in real time based on audience response.
We evaluate our own delivery critically and implement specific improvements.
